headline: National security legislation reviewed by 0 civil society organisations
context: Three national security bills introduced in 2025 went through the legislative process without any formal consultation with civil society organisations, legal bodies, or academic experts.

cards.big: 3 security bills passed without consulting a single civil society organisation
cards.sub: Laws affecting fundamental rights enacted without any independent expert input

cards.big: No legislation requires public consultation before bills are tabled in Parliament
cards.sub: Government has full discretion to bypass stakeholder engagement entirely

cards.big: Security bills expanded police powers affecting 33 million citizens' daily rights
cards.sub: Laws impacting everyone drafted by a handful of officials behind closed doors

cards.big: South Korea's Legislative Research Service mandates public comment period for all bills
cards.sub: Structured public consultation is standard practice in comparable democracies

cards.big: Security legislation without consultation assumes only the government understands security threats
Should all bills affecting fundamental rights require mandatory public consultation?
